libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, API key, or system property is required. Use local Spring Boot ApplicationContextRunner-style tests with stub ChatModel/local beans rather than a real model provider. For Spring AI 2.0.0, do not use the removed spring.ai.chat.client.tool-calling.stream-tool-call-responses property or ChatClientBuilderProperties.ToolCalling.isStreamToolCallResponses(); the 2.0.0 ToolCalling properties expose enabled and advisorOrder.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the ChatClient, ChatModel, prompt, advisor, and chat response APIs that the auto-configuration configures; it is declared optional by the target artifact and is not reliably pulled into the generated test classpath.
Provides tool-calling auto-configuration and related beans needed to exercise the target auto-configuration's tool-calling branch; it is also an optional companion module.
The target artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module and its configuration classes depend on Spring Boot auto-configuration APIs.
ApplicationContextRunner is the normal lightweight way to exercise Spring Boot auto-configuration without starting an application or external services.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8744 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-client:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing RC2 test fails to compile against 2.0.0 because it asserts an API/property removed before the final release.

Because the target artifact declares important collaborators as optional, a plain generated scaffold may compile poorly or only cover metadata/resource loading rather than the actual auto-configuration behavior.
Keep Spring AI companion dependencies aligned to 2.0.0 and Spring Boot dependencies aligned to 4.1.0 to avoid mixed RC/final API drift.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot/Spring AI auto-configuration module whose main usable API depends on optional Spring AI and Spring Boot classes; meaningful tests need those optional/test support dependencies on the test classpath.
